nodes.title: Reading and Writing Workshop
nodes.description: Improvement of reading and writing skills through the analysis and discussion of increasingly complex texts -- literary, filmic, cultural. Prerequisite: ITAL 2200 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: by instructor permission; PACE students with permission and override; Instructor permission

nodes.title: Elementary Italian II
nodes.description: Continuation of ITAL 1100. Fundamentals of Italian composition, comprehension, pronunciation, speaking, reading, and writing in a cultural context. Classes are conducted in Italian and students engage in active use of the language. Credit not awarded for ITAL 1200 following receipt of credit for any ITAL course numbered ITAL 2100 or above. Prerequisite: ITAL 1100 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students; Credit not awarded for ITAL 1200 following receipt of credit for any ITAL course numbered ITAL 2100 or above.

nodes.title: Elementary Italian I
nodes.description: Fundamentals of Italian composition, comprehension, pronunciation, speaking, reading, and writing in a cultural context. Classes are conducted in Italian and students engage in active use of the language. No prior knowledge expected. Cannot be taken for credit after ITAL 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: No prior knowledge expected. Cannot be taken for credit after ITAL 1200.

nodes.title: Intermediate Italian I
nodes.description: Review of grammar, moving toward increased proficiency in composition, comprehension, pronunciation, speaking, reading, and writing. Emphasis on cultural context. Compositions, oral practice, reading. Students may take 1 ITAL course numbered between 2100 and 2109 for credit. Credit not awarded for ITAL 2100 following receipt of credit for any ITAL course numbered ITAL 2200 or above. Prerequisite: ITAL 1200 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Intermediate Italian II
nodes.description: Continuation of ITAL 2100. Grammar review, moving toward increased proficiency in composition, comprehension, pronunciation, speaking, reading, and writing. Emphasis on cultural context. More extensive and sophisticated readings and compositions than in ITAL 2100. Students may take 1 ITAL course numbered between 2200 and 2209 for credit. Credit not awarded for ITAL 2200 following receipt of credit for any ITAL course numbered ITAL 3110 or above. Prerequisite: ITAL 2100 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: equivalent; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.
